headline: "Parking's a rip-off"
subtext: "The Council has recently raised the parking fees in our area. As ratepayers, we are expected to pay for this new, improved rubbish infrastructure. The excuse given was that the extra funds would go towards improving car-parking spaces."
body:
In an unprecedented move, local Councillors have unanimously decided to increase the cost of parking at Northern Beaches' most popular spots.

"The decision was not easy, but after extensive deliberation and much debate over the correct spelling of 'rubbish', we felt it prudent to raise parking fees," said one Cr who preferred to remain anonymous. 

Council Chambers insiders reveal that the new rates will be implemented in three stages at The Steyne, The Corso, and B-Line locations.

"The aim is to discourage hordes of beachgoers from clogging up our beloved footpaths," explained a Council insider on condition of anonymity.

Local Ratepayer Fred Smith was less than thrilled with the decision. "I'm all for funding the maintenance of our pristine beaches, but I can't keep pace with this escalating parking moolah!" he grumbled.

The Remy Digest has received reports that beachside entrepreneurs have begun offering discounts at cafes and kiosks to customers who brave the exorbitant car parks.

Watch for updates on this escalating situation.

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.

classifieds: For Sale: Broken AI. Contact Admin.

mystic.prediction: As The Mystic residing on the Northern Beaches of Sydney, I foresee a mundane yet ominous message for Pisces: Your parking karma aligns poorly with today's surf commute; resist grabbing that last latte at the coffee roastery as a parking spot opens unexpectedly near the B-Line Bus route - perhaps related to Bin Chickens' territorial disputes. Real estate whispers hint that your dream home might finally manifest soon, so brace yourself for a newfound commitment to decor.
